profile.title: Profile
profile.description: Your name and the PIN used when switching to your profile.

displayName.description: Shown on your calendar, your avatar, and when switching profiles.

color.description: Tints your avatar and your events on the calendar (unless an event sets its own color).

pin.descriptionSet: An 8-digit PIN is required when switching to your profile.
pin.descriptionUnset: No PIN set — add one to lock your profile when switching.

set.title: Set PIN
set.description: Choose an 8-digit PIN to require when switching to your profile.

change.title: Change PIN
change.description: Enter your current PIN, then choose a new one.

remove.title: Remove PIN
remove.description: Enter your current PIN to remove it. Your profile won't be locked.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Personalize how Planner looks. Changes save to your profile and follow you across devices.

language.description: The interface language. Saved to your profile and applied across your devices.

palette.description: Pick the warm default or a Catppuccin flavor. A flavor restyles the whole app and sets its own light or dark mode.

theme.descriptionLocked: The Catppuccin flavor sets light or dark.
theme.description: Match your system, or always use light or dark.

accent.descriptionLocked: Used for buttons, highlights, and focus rings — shown in the active flavor's palette.
accent.description: Used for buttons, highlights, links, and focus rings.

tone.description: The temperature of backgrounds and borders behind your content.

calendarDisplay.title: Calendar
calendarDisplay.description: How your calendar reads. These also follow you across devices.

showInactive.label: Show inactive events in month view
showInactive.description: Inactive events (like sleep) show grayed out. Turn off to hide them in the cramped month grid — week and day always show them.

contextLabel.description: How a context block is labelled in the week and day grids: a title bar across the top, or a vertical label down the right edge.

successToasts.label: Show success notifications
successToasts.description: Brief confirmations after an action (like “Task created”). Turn off to mute them — errors and warnings always show.

preview.title: Preview
preview.heading: The quick brown fox
preview.secondary: Secondary text sits on the current surface tone.

timezone.title: Time zone
timezone.description: The whole calendar shows times in your zone. A shared event stays anchored to the same moment, so each person sees it in their own time.

primary.description: Event times, day boundaries, and the “now” line all render in this zone — even when you travel and your device clock changes.

secondary.description: Show a second clock alongside the primary in the week and day views — handy for a partner in another zone.

sleep.title: Sleep
sleep.description: Drives the sleep-cycle calculator and tonight's bedtime recommendation on the Insights Sleep tab. Private to you.

cycleLength.label: Sleep cycle length
cycleLength.description: One light–deep–REM pass — about 90 minutes on average, though it varies night to night, so cycle counts are estimates.

onsetLatency.label: Time to fall asleep
onsetLatency.description: Added between getting into bed and the first cycle.

targetCycles.label: Nightly target
targetCycles.descriptionShort: With your settings that's {inBed} in bed ({asleep} asleep) per night. Adults do best on 7–9 hours of sleep (AASM) — your target is on the short side.
targetCycles.description: With your settings that's {inBed} in bed ({asleep} asleep) per night. Adults do best on 7–9 hours of sleep (AASM).

category.label: Sleep category
category.none: None — count inactive events as sleep
category.description: When set, only this category's timed events count as your derived nights — other inactive blocks (focus time, commutes) stop reading as sleep.

nightWindow.description: Derived nights collect sleep between these wall-clock hours — the start on the evening before, the end on the wake day. Widen it if you sleep past noon or keep unusual hours.
